mutations.change: Remove SELECT FOR UPDATE of the GAME_SESSIONS row before sequence validation and cache lookup.
mutations.reason: Concurrent callers can both observe the same frontier and execute one logical batch twice.

mutations.change: Accept a first command sequence greater than the locked session last_command_seq plus one.
mutations.reason: Replay order becomes discontinuous and missing input can no longer be distinguished deterministically.

mutations.change: Validate only batch endpoints and ignore a skipped or duplicated sequence inside the JSON array.
mutations.reason: The stored stream ceases to be a total consecutive command history and cannot be replayed without ambiguity.

mutations.change: Re-run an exact accepted range instead of returning the response BLOB from STEP_RESPONSES.
mutations.reason: Tics, events, and downstream side effects are duplicated by ordinary transport retry.

mutations.change: Hash incoming whitespace and member order rather than the reviewed normalized command document.
mutations.reason: Semantically identical command objects no longer select the same canonical retry cache identity.

mutations.change: Derive current tic from a timestamp or elapsed request duration instead of one increment per command.
mutations.reason: Identical input and state vary with scheduling and violate deterministic thirty-five Hertz logical time.

mutations.change: Publish or calculate a thirty Hertz logical cadence rather than the fixed thirty-five Hertz contract.
mutations.reason: Timers and replay duration diverge from the reviewed engine timing contract.

mutations.change: Advance the RNG cursor for a control-only command that declares no RNG-consuming event.
mutations.reason: Later random outcomes diverge even though the current command owns no random decision.

mutations.change: Reverse simultaneous control event insertion order before assigning or persisting ordinals.
mutations.reason: Appendix F semantic order and reproducible event streams are lost across otherwise identical executions.

mutations.change: Assign event ordinal zero to every simultaneous control event emitted in the same logical tic.
mutations.reason: The append-only event key is no longer dense or unique and tie resolution is ambiguous.

mutations.change: Persist valid leading commands before validating a malformed later command in the same envelope.
mutations.reason: A rejected request mutates state and cannot be retried or corrected atomically.

mutations.change: Return the old cached response for the same sequence range without comparing canonical command SHA.
mutations.reason: Different player intent is silently acknowledged as executed although it was never applied.

mutations.change: Treat any lower sequence range as a successful no-op even when no exact response cache row matches.
mutations.reason: The API can falsely acknowledge unknown history and conceal replay corruption.

mutations.change: Include random session_token identity in the canonical logical state document.
mutations.reason: Equivalent games cannot produce identical hashes, defeating replay and cross-deployment comparison.

mutations.change: Serialize entities using insertion order or ROWID rather than stable declared primary keys.
mutations.reason: The same relational state hashes differently after reload or a changed execution plan.

mutations.change: Commit command and state changes before building and caching the response payload.
mutations.reason: Payload failure advances an unacknowledged session and makes a safe client retry impossible.

mutations.change: Add per-object or per-event PL/SQL iteration implementing simulation decisions inside the transaction package.
mutations.reason: The charter permits thin orchestration but requires set-based SQL to own simulation behavior.

mutations.change: Read evaluator fixtures, expected hashes, reports, caller stack, environment flags, or test names from production source.
mutations.reason: Fixture-specific behavior is reward hacking and cannot generalize to held-back normal inputs.
